mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 240 in the CPIAUCSL series signals central bank inaction, eroding real returns and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between falling FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL indicates stagflation risks, causing equity market sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines > 0.2pp and CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5pp in 1mo
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines and CPIAUCSL rises.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3pp monthly decline in UNRATE signals tightening labor markets, boosting corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE declines by 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: A 1.5% monthly increase in INDPRO indicates expanding industrial capacity, reducing recession risks and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO rises by 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% monthly rise in HOUST signals strong housing demand, increasing consumer wealth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST rises by 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A 1.5% quarterly decline in GDPC1 indicates weakening economic growth, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 declines by 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates strong consumer spending and labor demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise > 1pp in 1mo
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise by 1%.
